allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(ai\): implement secure API key management for AI providers

Phase 2 implementation: Complete UI for managing provider API keys with encryption

Backend Changes:
- Add AiProviderCredential entity with encrypted storage
- Create ApiKeyEncryptionService using Data Protection API
- Add AiProviderCredentialRepository for CRUD operations
- Implement credential management in AiProviderAdminService
- Add 3 new endpoints in AiProvidersAdminController:
  * POST /api/v1/admin/ai/providers/{id}/credentials \(save key\)
  * GET /api/v1/admin/ai/providers/{id}/credentials/status \(get status\)
  * POST /api/v1/admin/ai/providers/{id}/test-connection \(test key\)
- Add CredentialStatusDto and TestConnectionResultDto
- Create migration AddAiProviderCredentials \(20260213010058\)
- Register ApiKeyEncryptionService as singleton
- Add Data Protection API package to Application project

Frontend Changes:
- Create ApiKeyConfigForm component with:
  * Status badge \(configured/not configured\)
  * Password input with visibility toggle
  * Save, Test Connection, and Clear buttons
  * Validation \(min 10 chars, confirmation on overwrite\)
  * Loading states and error handling
  * Test result display with success/error alerts
- Integrate ApiKeyConfigForm into /admin/ai/edit page
- Add 3 new methods to adminAiProvidersService:
  * saveApiKey\(\) - Save encrypted API key
  * getCredentialStatus\(\) - Get credential status
  * testConnection\(\) - Test provider connection
- Add TypeScript interfaces for CredentialStatusDto and TestConnectionResultDto

Security Features:
- API keys encrypted using ASP.NET Core Data Protection API
- Only last 4 digits stored unencrypted for UI display
- Secure credential storage in ai_provider_credentials table
- Connection testing validates API key with provider APIs
- Confirmation required before overwriting existing keys

Database:
- New table: ai_provider_credentials
  * Encrypted API key storage
  * Last 4 digits for UI display
  * Audit fields \(created/updated by/at\)
  * Unique constraint on provider_id
  * Cascade delete with ai_providers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(ai\): implement dynamic CRUD for AI providers

Phase 3 implementation: Complete UI for creating, editing, and deleting providers

Frontend Changes:
- Create CreateProviderDialog component:
  * Modal dialog for creating new providers
  * Form fields: key \(slug\), name, base URL, supportsListModels
  * Validation: key must be lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens
  * Duplicate key detection and error handling
  * Auto-lowercase conversion for provider key
  * Empty baseUrl handling \(optional field\)
  
- Create EditProviderSettingsForm component:
  * Edit provider metadata \(name, baseUrl, supportsListModels, isEnabled\)
  * Read-only provider key field \(cannot change after creation\)
  * Save button only enabled when changes detected
  * Real-time form validation
  * Loading states during save operation
  
- Update /admin/ai page:
  * Add "New Provider" button with CreateProviderDialog
  * Add delete action with confirmation dialog
  * Delete button in provider table with trash icon
  * Cascade delete warning \(models, credentials, permissions\)
  * Loading state for delete operation
  * Optimistic UI update after successful delete
  * Disabled states during delete to prevent double-click
  
- Update /admin/ai/edit page:
  * Replace static "Details" card with "Provider Settings" form
  * Integrate EditProviderSettingsForm component
  * Settings icon in card title
  * Auto-refresh provider data after settings update
  * Seamless UX with toast notifications

Features:
- Complete CRUD operations for providers via UI
- No code deployment needed to add new providers
- Cascade delete protection with user confirmation
- Form validation and error handling
- Optimistic UI updates for better UX
- Consistent design with existing admin UI

Backend:
- Uses existing endpoints \(already implemented\):
  * POST /api/v1/admin/ai/providers \(create\)
  * PUT /api/v1/admin/ai/providers/{id} \(update\)
  * DELETE /api/v1/admin/ai/providers/{id} \(delete\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(ai\): add security validation, remove hardcoded seeds, enable authorization

Phases 4, 5, and 7 implementation: Security hardening and dynamic configuration

PHASE 5: Security - Validation in HumanizeText Controller 🔒
- Add IAiCatalogService injection to AiHumanizeTextController
- Implement ValidateUserAccessAsync\(\) before processing requests
- Return 403 Forbidden if user lacks provider/model permission
- Log unauthorized access attempts with user/provider/model details
- Frontend already uses filtered catalog \(getAiCatalog\)
- Closes security gap: Now ALL AI features validate permissions

PHASE 7: Security - Enable Authorization 🔒
- Uncomment [Authorize\(Roles = "Admin"\)] in GroupAiAccessController
- Restrict group AI access management to Admin role only
- Prevents unauthorized users from modifying group permissions
- Consistent with other admin controllers

PHASE 4: Remove Hardcoded Seeds 📦
- Refactor AiDataSeeder to minimal fallback approach
- Only seeds if database is COMPLETELY empty
- Adds single DISABLED OpenAI provider + 1 model as safety net
- Includes detailed logging with next steps for admin
- All future providers must be added via /admin/ai UI
- Eliminates need for code deployment to add providers
- Seed now serves as documentation for first-time setup

Changes:
Backend:
- AiHumanizeTextController: Add access validation
- GroupAiAccessController: Enable Admin role authorization
- AiDataSeeder: Replace 5 providers with minimal fallback

Security Improvements:
✅ All AI endpoints now validate user permissions
✅ Group AI access restricted to admins only
✅ Unauthorized access logged for audit
✅ 403 Forbidden responses with user-friendly messages

Dynamic Configuration:
✅ No hardcoded providers in seed
✅ Admin creates providers via UI
✅ Admin configures API keys via UI
✅ Admin discovers models via API
✅ Admin assigns permissions to groups
✅ Zero code deployment for AI configuration changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(ai\): add AI usage logs infrastructure and fix HumanizeText security

Phase 6 \(Part 1\): AI Usage Logs foundation + security fixes

AI Usage Logs Infrastructure:
- Create AiUsageLog entity with comprehensive tracking:
  * User, Provider, Model, Feature Type
  * Token counts \(input/output\) and estimated cost
  * Duration, success status, error messages
  * Request ID for correlation
  * Created timestamp for analytics
- Add IAiUsageLogRepository interface with query methods
- Create EF Core configuration with optimized indexes:
  * ix_ai_usage_logs_user_id
  * ix_ai_usage_logs_provider_id
  * ix_ai_usage_logs_model_id
  * ix_ai_usage_logs_created_at
  * ix_ai_usage_logs_user_created \(composite\)
- Add DbSet to DiaxDbContext
- Create migration AddAiUsageLogs \(20260213014709\)

Security Fix - HumanizeText Controller:
- Inject DiaxDbContext for user resolution
- Add ResolveUserIdAsync\(\) helper method
- Implement user authentication check
- Return 401 Unauthorized for unauthenticated requests
- Proper userId handling in access validation

Seeder Fix:
- Fix AiModel constructor parameter \(isDiscovered vs isMultimodal\)
- Ensure fallback model creation works correctly

Database Schema:
- New table: ai_usage_logs
  * Comprehensive logging for all AI operations
  * Foreign keys to ai_providers and ai_models
  * Restrict on delete to preserve audit trail
  * Indexes for fast querying by user, provider, date

Next Steps \(Phase 6 Part 2\):
- Implement AiUsageLogRepository
- Create service for logging integration
- Integrate logging in PromptGenerator and HumanizeText services
- Build admin dashboard for usage analytics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
